--- SOURCE 1: Review of battery thermal management systems in electric vehicles ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1364032123010298

SUMMARY:
This paper reviews how heat is generated across a li-ion cell as well as the current research work being done on the four main battery thermal management types.

SUMMARY:
A Comprehensive Review of Thermal Management Methods and Ideal System Design for Improved Electric Vehicle Battery Pack Performance and Safety - Murugan - 2025 - Energy Science & Engineering - Wiley Online Library The thermal management system (TMS) of lithium-ion battery (LIB) packs is very critical in electric vehicle (EV) applications in terms of affecting performance and lifespan as well as impacting safety. They addressed the challenges associated with high-power charging, fast discharging, and operating conditions in EVs. Furthermore, the article highlighted the emerging techniques and future research directions for enhancing TMS for practical LBIs in EVs. The evolution of battery thermal models and thermal management techniques has been reviewed by Wang et al.

ABSTRACT
--------

The scientific aim of the study is to propose a comprehensive review of thermal management systems (TMSs) used in electric vehicle (EV) battery packs on matters pertaining to performance enhancement, improvements in safety, and reliability. This includes the various thermal management strategies, addressing some of the problems posed by the dynamic nature of operating conditions, and evaluating emerging TMS technologies. From this aspect, the problem of this research focused on the description of a detailed insight into the efficiencies of TMSs inside an EV, pointing to the impacts of various cooling mechanisms, mostly liquid cooling, air cooling, and phase-change materials. The research study further evaluates the integration of TMS in vehicle design and its effects on battery lifespan, charging speeds, and environmental impacts. The benefits, disadvantages, and specific applications of each method are discussed about EVs. Taking into consideration the fast charging, high-power, and environmental effects, further discussion is made on the specific challenges that come with dynamic operating conditions of EVs. This is shown through the industry's constant pursuit to develop in this critical area through the discovery of novel technologies, including predictive control algorithms and superior thermal materials. It discusses in depth how heat management is integrated into the general vehicle design and how this impacts battery lifespan, charging speed, and range. In conclusion, it is a source of material for research scholars, engineers, and policymakers in charge of developing EVs by synthesizing what already exists, highlighting trends at current times, and outlining possible future directions in the continuum of optimizing TMS for the next generation of driving automobile transportation batteries.

1 Introduction
--------------

The thermal management system (TMS) of lithium-ion battery (LIB) packs is very critical in electric vehicle (EV) applications in terms of affecting performance and lifespan as well as impacting safety. Although tremendous developments have been made concerning the thermal management of batteries, it is still necessary to find out the critical issues with the present system. Lu et al. [[1](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0001)] discussed the diverse aspects of the battery management system (BMS), which encompasses the battery modeling, state-of-charge (SOC) estimation, monitoring of state-of-health (SoH), thermal management, and concerns of safety. Challenges and opportunities in the management of EV batteries have been explained, and importance has been given to effective BMS determines the performance, lifespan, and safety of the batteries. It also gave insight into the development of emerging technologies and future directions in LIB management for EVs. Ye et al. [[2](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0002)] have carried out a numerical analysis of the thermal management aspects, which included mechanisms of heat generation, thermal models, cooling methods, and thermal management strategies. They addressed the challenges associated with high-power charging, fast discharging, and operating conditions in EVs. Furthermore, the article highlighted the emerging techniques and future research directions for enhancing TMS for practical LBIs in EVs. The evolution of battery thermal models and thermal management techniques has been reviewed by Wang et al. [[3](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0003)]. To do an accurate battery thermal analysis, the thermal effects of LIBs, including thermal runaway (TR) and reaction in cold conditions, have been analyzed. Additionally, heat generation approaches were deliberated. Furthermore, an assessment of the BMS tactics now employed by automotive suppliers has been conducted to pinpoint the significant obstacles and key gaps that exist between research and practice. An et al. [[4](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0004)] merely studied the impact of temperature on aging and capacity fading of battery packs of EVs. They analyzed the electrode structure, which includes the thickness, porosity, and size of the particles. It was discovered that each of them significantly affects how much heat the battery produces. To progress exterior heat dissipation, details of many TMS, namely, air-based, liquid-based, phase-change material (PCM)–based, and heat pipe–based, were examined and contrasted. The cooling requirements and applications should guide the choice of BMS solutions; liquid cooling was recommended as the best approach for comprehensive battery packs that are charged or drained at advanced C-rates and in environments with high temperatures. Wu et al. [[5](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0005)] discussed the three temperature-related effects on battery performance to be low, high, and differential temperatures. The discussion of the BMS focused mostly on temperature management techniques and battery modeling approaches. In addition, a thorough analysis of systems based on liquid was provided in terms of direct and indirect contact modes. Additionally, they covered the advancement in heat transfer fluid and liquid channel architecture that aims to improve overall thermal performance. Because of its high heat transfer efficiency, the heat pipe–based BMS was practical and efficient when combined with the liquid–gas phase-change process. Zhao et al. [[6](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0006)] presented the discussion on the need for efficient heat transfer in the battery pack, along with the utmost importance of uniform temperature distribution. The author also presented various cooling strategies. These include air-, liquid-, and PCM-based cooling. The authors have carried out a comparative analysis of merits and demerits based on cooling efficiency, energy consumption, cost, and practical implementation challenges. Furthermore, through the research paper, other advanced cooling techniques were portrayed, which include micro-channel cooling, jet impingement cooling, and heat pipe cooling, among others, showing an extensive potential for higher efficiency through the cooling mechanism. The discussion on integration with BMS resulted in improved thermal control in addition to an enhancement factor in safety.

These critical aspects are in need of further research and development for improvement in the TMS of LIB packs for EV applications. Cooling efficiency can be enhanced, uniform temperature distribution can be assured, TR can be prevented, energy efficiency optimized, and cost-effectiveness can be considered in battery thermal management systems (BTMSs) for making EVs safer, reliable, and long-lasting.

Briefly, the article provides a novel perspective on the existing literature in the following areas: (I) Advancements in TMSs, (II) Integration of Emerging Technologies, (III) Focus on Technology Evolution and Market Impact, and (IV) Safety and Environmental Considerations.

2 Thermal Management Strategies
-------------------------------

Thermal management strategies play a vital role in the optimization of the success and safety of EV battery packs. These include active cooling, passive cooling, and thermal insulation. Active cooling systems like liquid cooling can rapidly dissipate heat during charging and discharging cycles. On the other hand, passive cooling methods like PCMs regulate temperature without further energy input. The operational temperature levels stay stable due to thermal insulation; hence, there is low exposure to overheating or TR. With the above strategy combinations, EV battery packs will enhance their performances and safety aspects. Below are some of the most influential thermal management strategies:

_Cooling system efficiency:_ One of the major problems in managing battery temperatures is the achievement of efficient cooling. Most EVs rely on liquid cooling systems in controlling the temperatures in the battery. In reality, though, the efficiency of the cooling system could easily become a critical bottleneck when it is really in need during peak hours with activities like fast charging or massive use. Advanced heat transfer material or PCM and so on must be designed and applied to increase dissipative heat and subsequently also increase cooling efficiency.

_Uniform temperature distribution:_ Uniform temperature profiles are needed in the pack to get good performance and life. In the case of uneven temperature profiles, locally formed hotspots can severely damage certain battery cells due to a lack of uniform aging of these cells, greatly lowering overall battery capacity. Effective conduction and spreading within the pack are rather critical in keeping cell temperatures below some limit. Enhanced packing designs may be realized in combination with optimum layouts of the cooling channels and thermal interface material that facilitate better temperature homogenization.

_TR prevention:_ TR is a very serious safety concept in LIBs. It is initiated when the temperature rises, and TR can lead to an uncontrollable, self-sustaining reaction in the battery pack with consequent overheating, venting, or explosion. Such events should receive a maximum amount of attention at the stage of prevention and mitigation. Advanced thermal monitoring features along with safety features within the BMS, such as active cooling, thermal sensors, and protective layers, are required to provide for the effective detection and management of potential situations with TR.

_Thermal modeling (TM) and simulation:_ TM and simulation performance is one of the critical parts in the optimization of TMS. However, the complex mechanisms of heat transfer within the battery pack cannot be captured easily. Models that account for cell heterogeneity, heat generation during high-rate charging, and transient thermal behavior can enhance the strategy of thermal management and optimization of system design for higher performance and safety.

_Environmental impact and energy efficiency:_ The TMS must consider its environmental performance and efficiency in terms of energy use. Traditional air cooling technologies rely on massive mechanical equipment, which is less than optimal and reduces the average efficiency of the vehicle by considerable means. Exploring alternative means of cooling, for example, using passive cooling methods or exploiting waste heat, can mean reduced footprints and improvements in total energy efficiency for the TMS.

_Cost considerations:_ Among the most critical factors involved in accepting EVs is cost. The TMS will be cost-effective, sacrificing neither performance nor safety. Cost-effective cooling solutions are to be developed through low-cost material usage and streamlined processes aimed at reducing the total TBS TMS cost.

_Integration with vehicle design:_ The whole vehicle architecture must be appropriately integrated with the TMS system. The system has to be compact and of minimum weight so that the overall performance and efficiency of the range are not heavily influenced. Therefore, the process of integration and finding a better fit has to be collaborated on at various stages by all manufacturers of batteries and the manufacturers of vehicles with TMS.

### 2.1 Cooling System Efficiency

One of the factors that enhance the ideal performance and also durability of the vehicle battery pack is the efficiency of the cooling system in a battery of EVs. The cooling arrangement manages temperatures of battery chambers to stop them from overheating, which normally occurs during a charging and discharge cycle. Coolers work efficiently because very high temperatures accelerate degradation by batteries. Common cooling types are through air and through liquids. In air cooling, fans or natural convection is used. However, in liquid cooling, there is a circulating coolant through the battery pack. Various cooling systems of EV battery packs are illustrated in Figure[1](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-fig-0001).

[![Image 6: Details are in the caption following the image](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/cms/asset/b76339f2-5aeb-413f-96cf-3702616c8d1f/ese32081-fig-0001-m.png)](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/cms/asset/c453e573-7a28-4869-89e9-72e8de99919c/ese32081-fig-0001-m.jpg)

Various cooling systems of electric vehicle battery packs. PCM, phase-change material.

Air cooling is a common method used for thermal management in EV battery packs. This approach typically involves circulating air around the battery cells to disintegrate heat produced during charging and discharging [[7](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0007)]. One way to implement air cooling is through forced convection, where fans or blowers are used to direct air over the surface of the battery cells or through channels within the pack. This helps remove heat efficiently and maintain optimal operating temperatures. Air cooling systems are relatively simple and cost-effective compared with other methods like liquid cooling [[8](https://scijournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/ese3.2081#ese32081-bib-0008)]. However, they may have limitations in extreme temperature conditions or high-power applications where additional cooling capacity is required.

## KEY MARKET INSIGHTS

![Play Audio](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/accets/frontend/img/Play_gif.gif)

The global thermal management system market size was valued at USD 56.72 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow from USD 59.73 billion in 2024 to USD 95.64 billion by 2032, exhibiting a CAGR of 6.1% during the forecast period. Asia Pacific dominated the thermal management system market with a market share of 57.49% in 2023.

Thermal management refers to the tools and techniques used to keep a system within its operating temperature. In electronic devices, thermal management usually dissipates excess heat to prevent overheating. Most electronic systems generate heat due to which sensitive internal components can be damaged in case excess thermal energy builds up inside the electronic device. Similarly, electronics can be damaged by excessive exposure to high temperatures in the outdoor environment.

Engineers use thermal management solutions in various industries, including automotive, aerospace, [batteries](../battery-market-105615), data centers, and others. Temperature control can be a complex and critical aspect of any design. Engineers carefully consider the unique characteristics of each system to optimize the efficiency, durability, and longevity of its application. Rising demand of thermal systems across vehicles, aircraft, consumer electronics, and medical equipment will catalyze the market growth during forecast period.

Global markets experienced little impact from the COVID-19 pandemic. Due to total or partial lockdowns in many countries around the world, aviation and defense services were classified as non-essential and closed. This caused production disruptions and reduced sales in the defense and aerospace sectors. This had a negative impact on the growth of the global thermos systems market during the COVID-19 pandemic period.

## **Thermal Management System Market Trends**

***Hybrid Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) Shielding and Thermal Management to Drive Market Development***

Several industries are shifting towards higher frequency applications for navigation. Two main examples of this are 5G telecommunications for higher datax` rates and automotive radar for higher resolution detection. At higher frequencies, electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding with traditional methods becomes more difficult. Combining EMI shielding and thermal management effectiveness in a material becomes an attractive proposition.

5G deployment is well underway, but infrastructure for the highest frequency wave (>24 GHz) is still in its infancy. Most of the [5G infrastructure](../industry-reports/5g-infrastructure-market-100869) in 2022 is still in lower frequency bands; below 6 GHz. Moreover, car radar is also one of the main components of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) and a necessary technology for future autonomous vehicles. Early automotive radar units mostly used the 24 GHz frequency. Since then, the market has mostly moved beyond 77 GHz.

The compact nature of the higher signal device presents challenges for both thermal management and EMI shielding. Several companies have come up with new thermal interference solutions that also have EMI protection. Many players, such as Henkel, Kitagawa, and Schlegel have released thermal interference pads that also act as EMI shields. Such developments will guide the market’s growth during the forecast period.

## **Thermal Management System Market Growth Factors**

***Growing Demand of Electric Vehicles in Commercial Sector Boost the Growth of the Market***

[Electric vehicle (EV)](../industry-reports/electric-vehicle-market-101678) demand has been on the rise due to the need to lower greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and support sustainable transportation solutions. As EV sales have increased, so has the demand for EV Thermal Systems. The Thermal system maintains the operating temperatures of the battery, the motor, and various sensitive electronic components.

Good thermal management is important to improve vehicle performance, extend battery life, and ensure the safety and reliability of an electric vehicle. Battery technology has advanced significantly in recent years, and batteries have higher densities and faster charging speeds. This has increased the demand for advanced heating systems.

For instance, in February 2022, Borgwarner Inc. has been awarded a contract to supply a high-voltage coolant heater (HVCH) for use in BMW Group's all-electric iX and i4 architectures. The solution controls the heating of the thermal system and battery cabin, which significantly increases the range and reliability of the battery.

***Growing Demand of Consumer Electronics Boost the Market Growth***

All electronic components, from microprocessors to advanced power converters, generate heat, and dissipating that heat is essential for their optimal and reliable operation. As electronic design enables higher efficiency in smaller packages, thermal load distribution becomes a critical design factor. Many of today's electronic devices require more cooling than conventional metal heatsinks. A heat pipe is a compact and efficient heat management tool for such applications. For smaller devices where space is a major constraint, passive heat removal methods through heat pipes are essential for operational efficiency of the system.

According to UNCTAD, sale of consumer electronics and technology products will grow in coming years especially in the Asia Pacific market backed by reduction in inflation, value seeking consumers and markets across India, Indonesia, Vietnam, Malaysia, and other Southeast Asian countries.

## **RESTRAINING FACTORS**

***High Development and Power Cost of Thermal Management System to Hamper Market Growth***

The use of copper increases the cost of the system, which hikes the total cost of the product and negatively affects the growth of the market. The increasing complexity of electronics and other devices makes it difficult to integrate a thermal management system into such devices, which increases costs.

The need for efficient data centers has increased as the world continues to have a more prominent virtual presence. As the energy density increases, enhanced infrastructure is needed to keep everything running smoothly and efficiently. The cooling system is one of the most expensive parts of a data center. These components increase cooling costs in data centers. According to our research, 30-55% of a data center's energy consumption goes to power cooling and [ventilation systems](../ventilation-system-market-102834). Hence, data centers require high initial cost and face high operational cost during its work life. Such factors hamper the growth of the market.

***Rising Demand of Commercial and Passenger Vehicle led to Fastest Growth Rate of Automobile Segment***

Based on end user, the market is classified into [consumer electronics](../consumer-electronics-market-104693), naval & marine, industrial machineries, aviation, automobiles, medical equipment, agriculture equipment, construction equipment, and others. The automobiles segment has the largest share and fastest growing segment for 2024-2032 owing to increasing deliveries of EVs and other vehicles.

The naval & marine segment will show moderate growth during the forecast period. This growth can be attributed due to rising demand of naval fleet by defense sector of different nations.

### **By Equipment Analysis**

***Heat Exchangers Segment Held Highest Share Owing to its Major Application across Various Industries***

Based on the equipment, the market is segmented into heat exchangers, compressors, heat pipes and hoses, evaporators, pumps & control valves, accumulators & reservoirs, purge / fill systems, filters and motor controllers, and others. The heat exchangers segment dominated the market in 2023. The growth is attributed to the growing adoption of heat exchanger components in thermal management system.

Compressor segment holds significant share and will grow at moderate rate owing to rising demand across [Heating, Ventilation, and Cooling (HVAC) systems](../heating-ventilation-and-cooling-hvac-system-market-102664) for vehicle, aircraft and others.

### **By Technology Analysis**

***Active Cooling Segment Dominated the market due to its Key Features and Compactness***

By technology type, the market is classified into active cooling and passive cooling. The active cooling segment dominated the market in 2023 and is expected to grow at fastest rate during the forecast period owing to its advantages such as compactness and better cooling efficiency. Moreover, technological advancements in key components of liquid-cooled engines will create lucrative opportunities for market growth in the coming years.

The passive cooling segment is projected to show significant growth owing to rising demand across automotive, marine, and industrial sectors.

enriched_points.expanded_content: This report reviews various thermal management systems, focusing on lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicles, highlighting performance, safety, and efficiency improvements.
Essential thermal management techniques include active and passive cooling, thermal insulation, and advanced materials, crucial for optimizing battery temperature and enhancing performance.
Key challenges include managing high temperatures and preventing thermal runaway, while innovations focus on advanced cooling strategies and real-time monitoring systems for improved safety.
Future trends emphasize integrating smart technologies, enhancing cooling efficiencies, and developing sustainable materials to meet the growing demands of electric vehicles and consumer electronics.
